one_time_description: Forgiven debt from Kainai Market Place recognized as other revenue
economic_notes: Department manages economic development projects on Blood Tribe Reserve including KainaiLink LP joint venture with Altalink for wind power. Advances of $6,697,500 to 1757051 Alberta Ltd. for KainaiLink LP project bearing interest at prime plus 5.0% (11.70% at March 31, 2023). Kainai Marketplace grocery store building held as tangible capital asset.
notes: This is a departmental financial statement for Blood Tribe Economic Development only, not the full Blood Tribe band. Fiscal year end is March 31, 2023 (not 2024). A significant one-time item of $1,527,758 forgiven debt from Kainai Marketplace Ltd. was recognized as other revenue following settlement of BMO term loan. The Department uses modified equity method for investment in business enterprises including Kainai Marketplace Ltd. and 1757051 Alberta Ltd. (KainaiLink LP). Long-term debt was fully forgiven during the year (previously $2,247,841). Net financial assets of $6,473,821 per Statement of Changes in Net Financial Assets. Advances to Blood Tribe entities of $737,572 and advances from Blood Tribe entities of $6,697,500 represent significant intercompany balances. ISC funding includes Block ($844,269) and Fixed ($50,015) contributions totaling $894,284; deferred revenue of $887,917 relates to ISC Indigenous Community Business Fund.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
